title: Buttery Spanish Chicken Paella
description: A rich, buttery paella made with chicken and rice, perfect for a single, diabetic-friendly dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil and butter in a deep skillet or paella pan over medium heat until the butter foams.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, paprika, and saffron until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Pour in the diced tomatoes and cook until the mixture thickens slightly, 2–3 minutes.
Add the chicken slices, season with salt and pepper, and cook until they turn pink, about 4 minutes.
Stir in the rice, ensuring each grain is coated with the sauce.
Pour in the chicken broth, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes.
Add the peas, cover, and continue cooking until the rice is tender and liquid is absorbed, about 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and let rest, covered, for 5 minutes.
Fluff the paella with a fork, sprinkle chopped parsley on top, and serve hot.
